reasoning: The baseline spread on 2025-08-31 was 1.57, which was a local peak likely driven by month-end weekend adjustment (having jumped from 1.55 on Aug 28-29). In the short term (7-30 days), the spread is very likely to revert to its recent intrinsic track around 1.51-1.55, making an increase (value > 1.57) less probable (estimated at ~0.29 for Sept 7 and ~0.39 for Sept 30). For longer horizons (90 and 180 days), current spreads are near historical lows (10th percentile over the last year, compared to an average of ~1.75). The asymmetric risk over the long term favors spread widening, primarily due to mean reversion or potential macroeconomic shocks. This causes the probability of an increase to drift upwards, approaching and slightly exceeding 50% at the 6-month horizons (0.52 for Feb 2026), cleanly aligning with the requirement that uncertainty increases and probabilities move closer to 0.5 for distant dates.
